rule.desc: Use `Final` for constants that should never be reassigned.
rule.rationale: Final prevents accidental modification of constants, documents immutability intent.

example.code:
# Good - constants are Final DEFAULT_DATE_FORMAT: Final = "%Y-%m-%d" MAX_RETRIES: Final = 3 API_BASE_URL: Final = "https://api.example.com"
# In code date_str = date.strftime(DEFAULT_DATE_FORMAT) # OK
# Attempt to reassign DEFAULT_DATE_FORMAT = "%m/%d/%Y" # mypy error: Cannot assign to final name
# Good - Final class attribute class Config: DATABASE_URL: Final = "postgresql://localhost/db" TIMEOUT: Final = 30
# Bad - mutable constant (no Final) DEFAULT_DATE_FORMAT = "%Y-%m-%d" # ... later in code ... DEFAULT_DATE_FORMAT = "%m/%d/%Y" # Accidentally modified, no error!
